_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. Result of the colonoscopy with piecemeal EMR (2026-07-14) that PT-049's post-procedure follow-up encounter is following up FROM.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy with endoscopic mucosal resection (EMR)
procedure.indication: Surveillance colonoscopy (prior sigmoid adenoma); large laterally spreading sigmoid lesion identified for resection
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve intubation)

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with piecemeal endoscopic mucosal resection. INDICATION: surveillance colonoscopy with a large laterally spreading sigmoid lesion identified for resection; history of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation on apixaban (held 48h pre-procedure per anticoagulation plan) and hypertension. SEDATION: monitored anesthesia care (propofol). PREP: adequate (BBPS 8/9). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum. In the sigmoid colon a single 22 mm laterally spreading, sessile/granular lesion (Paris 0-IIa + Is) was identified. Submucosal injection of lifted saline/methylene blue solution produced a good lift with no non-lifting sign. The lesion was removed by piecemeal endoscopic mucosal resection using a stiff snare; all visible tissue was resected and specimens retrieved for histology. The post-resection defect was inspected: several non-bleeding visible vessels were noted at the margin and base and were treated prophylactically with through-the-scope clips (5 clips placed). No immediate intraprocedural bleeding at completion. Scattered sigmoid diverticula without stigmata of bleeding. The remainder of the examined colon and terminal ileum were normal. IMPRESSION: 22 mm sigmoid laterally spreading lesion removed by piecemeal EMR; prophylactic clip closure of the resection base. Histology pending. Delayed post-polypectomy bleeding precautions given; apixaban to resume post-procedure Day 2 (2026-07-16) per plan.

findings.finding: 22 mm laterally spreading sessile granular lesion (Paris 0-IIa+Is)
findings.action_taken: piecemeal endoscopic mucosal resection after submucosal lift; specimens retrieved

findings.finding: Post-EMR mucosal defect with non-bleeding visible vessels

pathology.report: Sigmoid colon, piecemeal EMR: tubulovillous adenoma with focal high-grade dysplasia. No definite submucosal invasion identified in the fragments examined; deep and lateral margins cannot be assessed due to the piecemeal nature of the resection. No high-risk features of invasive carcinoma (no lymphovascular invasion, no poor differentiation) seen in submitted material.
pathology.diagnosis: Tubulovillous adenoma with focal high-grade dysplasia (piecemeal EMR, margins not assessable)

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-polypectomy and delayed-bleeding precautions reviewed (call clinic or present to ED for black stools, rectal bleeding, lightheadedness, or abdominal pain). Given resumption of apixaban on Day 2, delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage risk explicitly counseled.

followup.recommendation: Communicate high-grade dysplasia pathology result to patient; document that piecemeal EMR margins are not assessable
Schedule early follow-up colonoscopy to evaluate/biopsy the EMR scar for residual/recurrent adenoma (piecemeal resection of high-grade lesion)
Resume apixaban on post-procedure Day 2 (2026-07-16) per anticoagulation plan if no bleeding; counsel on delayed bleeding warning signs
Monitor for delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage (Day 1-14 window), heightened risk on resumed apixaban after 22 mm EMR

planted_followup_items.description: Piecemeal EMR pathology shows tubulovillous adenoma with FOCAL HIGH-GRADE DYSPLASIA; margins not assessable due to piecemeal resection. Must be communicated to patient and must drive a short-interval scar surveillance plan.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with high-grade dysplasia result, document, and book early EMR-scar surveillance colonoscopy

planted_followup_items.description: Early follow-up colonoscopy to inspect and biopsy the sigmoid EMR scar for residual/recurrent adenoma after piecemeal resection of a 22 mm high-grade lesion
Surveillance interval must be set based on the high-grade dysplasia / large piecemeal-resected lesion (short-interval scar check, then risk-adjusted ongoing surveillance)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document surveillance interval in chart and communicate to patient

planted_followup_items.description: Delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage watch (Day 1-14) after 22 mm piecemeal EMR with visible vessels, markedly elevated by apixaban resumption on Day 2. NOTE: this risk was subsequently realized as a Day-4 delayed bleed (see follow-up encounter).
planted_followup_items.expected_action: counsel bleeding precautions; if bleeding occurs, hold apixaban, ED evaluation, and repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis

planted_followup_items.description: Apixaban 5 mg BID resumption on post-procedure Day 2 (2026-07-16) per anticoagulation plan — a confirmed active DOAC that is the principal driver of post-polypectomy bleeding risk and must not be de-prioritized
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm resumption timing and align with bleeding-risk monitoring / hold instructions if bleeding

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored. Profile: one_complication (Day-4 delayed post-EMR hemorrhage on resumed apixaban).

contacts.verbatim: Bit of cramping and a lot of gas today, and my backside feels a little tender. Is that normal after having the polyp taken out?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EMR insufflation cramping/bloating and minor anorectal tenderness on Day 1; no bleeding, no orthostatic symptoms
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; cramping, gas, and mild tenderness are expected and self-limited. Reinforce delayed-bleeding return precautions (black/tarry stools, rectal bleeding, lightheadedness, belly pain).
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms after insufflation; no red-flag features present.

contacts.verbatim: The sheet says to start my apixaban back today, Day 2. Am I really supposed to restart the blood thinner already, and when will I hear about the biopsy?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient confirming anticoagulation resumption timing and asking about pending pathology — answerable from the plan
contacts.expected_action: Confirm apixaban 5 mg BID resumes Day 2 (2026-07-16) per the anticoagulation plan since no bleeding; re-counsel on delayed-bleeding warning signs given resumed DOAC. Advise pathology and surveillance interval will be communicated by the GI team within the week.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the med_resume item and reinforces bleeding precautions on a re-anticoagulated patient.

contacts.verbatim: Since this morning I've had two really dark, almost black tarry stools, and every time I stand up the room tips and I get dizzy. I've been back on the apixaban since Thursday like they told me.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage — melena x2 plus orthostatic lightheadedness on Day 4 after 22 mm piecemeal EMR, on apixaban resumed Day 2. Classic delayed-bleed window with a hemodynamic symptom.
contacts.expected_action: Escalate to the ED now for resuscitation, CBC/type & screen, and hemodynamic monitoring; hold apixaban (no further doses until cleared) and discuss reversal per ED; notify the endoscopist and anticipate repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis.
contacts.rationale: Melena with orthostatic vital signs on a resumed DOAC after a large EMR is a red-flag delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage requiring emergent action, not a reassurance contact.
